mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations and reduce corporate borrowing costs, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of rate-cut months, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid acceleration in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and falling consumer income, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >60% of instances where unemployment rises >0.5pp, or signal Rank IC drops below 0.01 during high-volatility periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad-based contraction in consumer demand and credit-dependent spending, which compresses aggregate corporate revenues and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when either series is stable, or 60-day forward returns are positive in >50% of co-declining months.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in the index of industrial production reflect weakening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which directly reduces near-term corporate cash flows and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >55% of months with falling industrial production, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance during low-volatility periods.

mechanism_narrative: A widening gap between falling nominal interest rates and persistent inflation compresses real discount rates, which increases the present value of future equity cash flows and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive returns do not materialize when real rates compress, or the interaction term yields a Rank IC below 0.02 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in total nonfarm payrolls indicate expanding employment and rising household income, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months with payroll growth, or the signal out-of-sample R-squared falls below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly contraction in real GDP confirms a recessionary downturn that severely impairs corporate profitability and investor sentiment, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >60% of quarters with negative GDP growth, or the signal fails to achieve statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in industrial production and nonfarm payrolls signal a synchronized collapse in output and labor demand, which accelerates earnings downgrades and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces positive 5-day returns in >50% of co-declining months, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in walk-forward validation.
